description: Phase 1: Dynamic deck from server + expanded meme/situation content with categories. The app fetches memes and situations from the server API instead of using hardcoded deck.ts. Server stores the deck on its persistent volume (/data/deck.json). New memes (single-image emotion memes) and situations are added, organized by categories (films, work, absurd). Fallback to embedded deck.ts if server unreachable.
steps: Step 1: Create server/src/deck-store.ts - a module that manages the deck on the /data volume. On first start, seed from the current deck.ts data. Store as /data/deck.json with structure: { memes: MemeCard[], situations: Situation[], categories: Category[] } where Category = { id, name_ua, name_ru }. Provide CRUD functions (getDeck, addMemes, removeMemes, addSituations, etc.).

Step 2: Add server endpoints in server/src/index.ts: GET /api/deck (returns full deck: memes, situations, categories), GET /api/deck/memes (memes only), GET /api/deck/situations (situations only). Admin endpoints: POST /api/deck/memes (add memes), DELETE /api/deck/memes/:id, POST /api/deck/situations, DELETE /api/deck/situations/:id.

Step 3: Expand the deck content - add 50+ new single-image emotion memes (from imgflip - popular reaction memes that are single images with clear emotions, NOT multi-panel or text-template memes). Add 30+ new situations organized by categories: 'films' (movie-related), 'work' (office/job humor), 'absurd' (surreal situations). Assign category field to each situation.

Step 4: Modify the game engine to accept deck data as a parameter instead of importing from deck.ts directly. In MemKarti-cloud/apps/mobile-v2/src/game/lanGame.ts, change functions (startRound, drawCards) to accept a deck parameter { memes: MemeCard[], situations: Situation[] }. Keep backward compatibility - if no deck passed, use the hardcoded MEME_CARDS/SITUATIONS from deck.ts as fallback.

Step 5: Create MemKarti-cloud/apps/mobile-v2/src/game/deckClient.ts - a client module that fetches the deck from SERVER_URL/api/deck on app launch, caches it in memory. Export a useDeck() hook or getDeck() async function. Falls back to deck.ts if fetch fails.

Step 6: Update the solo screen (src/app/solo.tsx) and online screen (src/app/online.tsx) to fetch the deck on mount and pass it to the engine. For online mode, the server already has the deck - modify server/src/rooms.ts to use the dynamic deck from deck-store.ts instead of the hardcoded import.

Step 7: Update server/src/engine.ts and server/tsconfig.json to reflect the new deck parameter approach. The server's RoomManager should load the deck from deck-store on room creation and pass it to createLobby/startRound.
Step 8: Update the Dockerfile and server package.json if any new dependencies are needed. Ensure /data directory is used for deck persistence.
acceptance_criteria: Server exposes GET /api/deck returning memes, situations, and categories
App fetches deck from server on launch and uses it for gameplay
If server is unreachable, app falls back to hardcoded deck.ts
At least 150 memes total (current 105 + 50 new) in the deck
At least 150 situations total (current 120 + 30 new) with category tags
Categories include at minimum: films, work, absurd
Server typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit in server/)
Game engine still works for solo mode with dynamic deck
verification: Start server briefly and curl GET /api/deck to verify response structure
findings: Implemented successfully. Server deck-store seeds 160 memes (105 original + 55 new) and 156 situations (120 original + 36 new) with 4 categories (general, films, work, absurd). Docker build passes. Both server and app typechecks pass. The DeckData type is optional in createLobby - backward compatible. Online mode uses dynamic deck from deck-store; solo mode fetches from server and falls back to hardcoded. DATA_DIR env var controls storage path (default /data).
